mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuations by lowering discount rates and refinancing pressure, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign flips and falling FEDFUNDS is followed by negative returns in a majority of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations and raises recession odds, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes of rising unemployment deliver positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening nominal demand with improving labor income and retail spending, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS fails to outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in triggered joint-upturn periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO confirms factory and production acceleration that improves cyclical earnings expectations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its prior 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if crossings above the 3-month moving average are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the short-horizon hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A downturn in HOUST after an earlier surge signals fading housing-led demand and weaker cyclical momentum, which should turn S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months after a 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if post-peak HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal has no incremental explanatory power over time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real-rate repression and inflation tolerance support equity multiples, creating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not statistically better than either CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, a continuing decline in UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS signals improving income and consumption capacity, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months and PAYEMS rises
failure_condition: Reject if combined UNRATE declines and PAYEMS gains fail to outperform unconditional returns, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in most labor-repair episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A weak GDP trend combined with soft retail sales indicates broad demand contraction and lower forward earnings, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on the latest release while RSAFS is flat or negative versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail weakness is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered cases, or if the negative signal is indistinguishable from noise in rolling walk-forward tests.
